title: GENERAL LEGAL AID - 211info
text: LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON SALEM REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and representation for individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Eligibility: Income below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level. Must live in or have a legal issue in Marion or Polk Counties. Hours: Walk-in: Monday/Thursday 1pm-5pm, Tuesday/Wednesday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Attorney consultation by appointment. (503) 581-5265 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 581-5265 Toll-free (800) 359-1845 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for appointment. Appointments available Monday-Wednesday. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Case-specific papers should be brought to the appointment LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Marion and Polk Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday: 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Friday: 9am-noon TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Corner of Liberty Street and Trade Street SE Services: Comprehensive Family Law Services Criminal Record Expungement Assistance General Legal Aid Other Services Offered At This Location: LEGAL SERVICES - SENIOR LAW PROJECT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
